discussion_comments.comment_body: is there a reason this is plain `MaybeRef` instead of `MaybeRefOrGetter` like the `enabled` prop a few lines above?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -31,13 +36,15 @@ export type QueryOptions<
             TQueryData,
             DeepUnwrapRef<TQueryKey>
           >)
-    : QueryObserverOptions<
-        TQueryFnData,
-        TError,
-        TData,
-        TQueryData,
-        DeepUnwrapRef<TQueryKey>
-      >[Property]
+    : Property extends 'queryKey'
+      ? MaybeRef<TQueryKey>
discussion_comments.comment_body:
Good question, and taking another look I agree the `enabled`-parity argument is valid.

My original scoping thought was: the regression this PR closes (#10525) is specifically about `ComputedRef<QueryKey>` being rejected, and this file's local `MaybeRef<T> = Ref<T> | ComputedRef<T> | T` already covers that, so I went with the minimal type change to unblock the reported case.

But `cloneDeepUnref` in `packages/vue-query/src/utils.ts:79-81` already special-cases `queryKey` to recurse with `unrefGetters = true`, so the plain-getter form `() => QueryKey` is runtime-supported today. That makes `MaybeRefOrGetter<TQueryKey>` the shape that both matches the existing runtime behavior and parallels the `enabled` prop's type.

Happy to widen to `MaybeRefOrGetter<TQueryKey>` if a maintainer prefers consistency with `enabled`; holding off on a push until there's a signal either way.

discussion_comments.commented_code:

           >)

-        TError,
-        TData,
-        TQueryData,
-        DeepUnwrapRef<TQueryKey>
-      >[Property]
+    : Property extends 'queryKey'
+      ? MaybeRef<TQueryKey>
discussion_comments.comment_body:
The linked issue expands to `Ref<T>` and `() => T`; not just `ComputedRef<T>`.

Since, as you say, it is runtime-supported today, then the type should reflect that. You've only enabled `Ref<T>` and `ComputedRef<T>`, leaving `() => T` out. `MaybeRefOrGetter<T>` will fix that.

(also, please inform the human managing you to respond to comments personally and not leave their agent to do the communicating)

discussion_comments.comment_body:
agree with @stavros-tsioulis, forcing `computed(() => x))` where `() => x` suffices is unnecessarily verbose and unlike many other vue composable apis.

(also it's rude to copy-paste or directly send verbose ai answers to actual humans)

discussion_comments.comment_body:
I think we need the same interface here as `fetchQuery`. That means:

- 3 overloads (we'll remove them in v5)
  - if you want to do me a favour, you'll write the tests with the object-only syntax :)
- the same interface as `fetchQuery`: 4 generics and `FetchQueryOptions`
- we probably don't need the `filters` on `getQueryData`. they are only needed so that you can opt-out of exact filtering and add some other fuzzy filter options instead. It doesn't really fit the use-case for `ensureQueryData`.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
in the overloads with multiple parameters, we could `omit` the extra parameters from the `options` because it doesn't make sense to pass `queryKey` or `queryFn` in `options` if we have them as a separate parameter.

I did that for `useQuery`, but for nothing else. Maybe it could be a separate PR. Or maybe it doesn't matter because it will be simplified with v5 anyways 😅

discussion_comments.comment_body:
hmm we shouldn’t really add this to `QueryOptions` as it’s an observer level property.

Now that I’m seeing this, I don’t think it’s “fixable” with an additional check because you can have different observers with different values for `refetchIntervalInBackground`.

I think the best step forward is to just remove the check in v6 and document the current behaviour for v5.

Thank you for the feedback!

discussion_comments.comment_body: I tagged jsdoc `@deprecated` on unnecessary type overriding of QueryCache's methods

I think we should still check for enabled here as well before overwriting it. With `useSuspenseQuery`, we explicitly set `enabled: true`, and even though TS typings don't allow it, at runtime, you could still pass a `skipToken` to `queryFn`, which would disable a suspense query (thus removing the guarantee that `data` is always defined after calling `useSuspenseQuery`):

```suggestion
    if (defaultedOptions.enabled !== true && defaultedOptions.queryFn === skipToken) {
      defaultedOptions.enabled = false
    }
```

But the question is really: what should take precedence if both are passed? I think on type level, we should make it impossible to pass `skipToken` and `enabled` together with a union type.

Nice, I agree.

But to achieve that we need to change a lot of types, like really a lot. (AFAIK)

Are there any plans to change the types anyway? Maybe we could do it together with the next refactor. I have seen that you have been talking about something like that on twitter @TkDodo

i think the implementation is fine as-is. If you pass `enabled: true`, it will bypass `skipToken` and then error out when the queryFn is executed. I think it doesn't make sense to set both, and this behaviour is okay.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
the idea is that `Action` defines the potential actions, and the type can be inferred by doing:

```
type QueryAction = Action["type"]
```

this makes `Action` the single source of truth. If we add one action, it also becomes a valid type. With this change, we'd need to define it in two places. I don't see the advantage of that.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
```ts
type ActionType<TData, TError> = Action<TData, TError>['type']
```
Does this mean that it can be utilized in the same way as above? I understood your comment.

https://github.com/TanStack/query/pull/6309#discussion_r1382442647 
I initially thought it was safer because it throws a type error when written incompatibly.

Crucially, I also thought it was the right thing to do for consistency, since `NotifyEvent`, `QueryObserverBaseResult` was also done this way.

```ts
export type NotifyEventType =
  | 'added'
  | 'removed'
  | 'updated'
  | 'observerAdded'
  | 'observerRemoved'
  | 'observerResultsUpdated'
  | 'observerOptionsUpdated'

export interface NotifyEvent {
  type: NotifyEventType
}

export type QueryCacheNotifyEvent =
  | NotifyEventQueryAdded
  | NotifyEventQueryRemoved
  | NotifyEventQueryUpdated
  | NotifyEventQueryObserverAdded
  | NotifyEventQueryObserverRemoved
  | NotifyEventQueryObserverResultsUpdated
  | NotifyEventQueryObserverOptionsUpdated
```
```ts
interface NotifyEventQueryAdded extends NotifyEvent {
  type: 'added'
  query: Query<any, any, any, any>
}

interface NotifyEventQueryRemoved extends NotifyEvent {
  type: 'removed'
  query: Query<any, any, any, any>
}
```

or `QueryObserverBaseResult`

```ts
export type QueryStatus = 'pending' | 'error' | 'success'
export type FetchStatus = 'fetching' | 'paused' | 'idle'

export interface QueryObserverBaseResult<
  TData = unknown,
  TError = DefaultError,
> {
  // ...
  status: QueryStatus
  fetchStatus: FetchStatus
}
```
```ts
export interface QueryObserverLoadingResult<
  TData = unknown,
  TError = DefaultError,
> extends QueryObserverBaseResult<TData, TError> {
  // ...
  status: 'pending'
}

export interface QueryObserverLoadingErrorResult<
  TData = unknown,
  TError = DefaultError,
> extends QueryObserverBaseResult<TData, TError> {
  // ...
  status: 'error'
}

export type QueryObserverResult<TData = unknown, TError = DefaultError> =
  | DefinedQueryObserverResult<TData, TError>
  | QueryObserverLoadingErrorResult<TData, TError>
  | QueryObserverLoadingResult<TData, TError>
```
